retry_reason: Failure indicates the device is not found or has been disconnected — both conditions are permanent; retrying will not make the device reappear.
contract_summary: Lookup-and-get wrapper: acquires a reference on the tty_port of the acm device identified by minor number, but only if the device exists and is not disconnected. The caller MUST check the return value: a non-NULL pointer carries an additional reference that must be released via tty_port_put when no longer needed. A NULL return means the device is absent or gone — the caller must not use the pointer. The function handles all internal locking and disconnect detection to guarantee safe access.

check.reason: tty_port_get(&acm->port) increments the kref of acm->port, and acm is returned to the caller. The access path acm.port.kref.refcount correctly starts from the returned acm variable, matching the callee's path port.kref.refcount with prefix acm. Type chain acm--tty_port--kref--refcount_t is consistent in segment count (4) and types.
